title: Document Permission Controls for Shared Accounts
description:
## Context
Permission labels on the shared account settings page were unclear. This ticket documents what each permission controls and proposes improvements.

## Permission Reference

| Permission | Controls |
|---|---|
| **List Scorecard** | Shared user can see the list of scorecards/objects on the dashboard. If unchecked, the list is hidden when viewing that account. |
| **Create Scorecard** | Shared user can add/remove scorecards. Section is hidden if unchecked. |
| **Edit Scorecard** | Shared user can edit scorecard details. |
| **Delete Scorecard** | Shared user can delete scorecards from the shared account. |
| **Allow Scorecard Mapping** | "Click to Map" appears as a link if checked; plain text if unchecked. |
| **Create Source Object** | Shared user can see the "Add Source Object" link. |
| **Edit Scorecard Source Object** | Shared user can edit source objects. |
| **Unlink Scorecard Source Object Meta** | Shared user can remove/unlink source objects. |
| **List Metric** | Shared user can see the metric list. |
| **Create Metric** | Shared user can add new metrics. Section hidden if unchecked. |
| **Edit Metric** | Shared user sees the pencil icon and can update metrics. |
| **Delete Metric** | Shared user sees the delete icon and can delete metrics. |

## Future Work
- Rename labels to be more end-user-friendly.
- Add info icons with descriptions.
- Determine which permissions should be admin/licensing-only vs. user-configurable.
acceptance_criteria: Each permission correctly controls the described UI element
Labels are candidates for future renaming
implementation_notes: Documentation task. Permissions were already implemented — this ticket maps each permission to its UI behavior for future label improvements.
comment_summary: Developer provided detailed descriptions of each permission with screenshots showing the controlled UI elements. Reporter confirmed the documentation.
